text.title: Drift Classification Taxonomy Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Weekly plan shows 12 differences; engineer runs apply; differences gone; nobody knows what changed.
antipattern.reason: No classification step; drift treated as 'something to apply'.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add classification step before apply. Each diff is a row in drift-classification.yaml. Apply only after class + fix_action recorded.

antipattern.description: On-call fixed prod via console at 3 AM; next apply reverted the fix; same incident re-occurred.
antipattern.note: Fix: Tag class = legitimate_emergency. 5-day deadline for backport PR. Pause apply on that resource until backport merged.

antipattern.description: Drift table has 'someone in slack said it's ok', 'i think we changed it', 'unknown';' analysis impossible.
antipattern.note: Fix: Enforce closed set via validate-drift-classification-taxonomy.py. Reject submissions with non-enum class.

antipattern.description: Cloud provider changed default for a property; team's apply silently overwrote with the old value; vendor docs say new default is required for compliance.
antipattern.reason: vendor_change drift treated as hand_edit; provider not bumped.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add provider-version-bump PR when class == vendor_change. Update HCL to express the new default explicitly.

antipattern.description: Drift queue has 80 items, oldest from 6 months ago.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add 30-min weekly triage. Items > 14 days untriaged escalate to engineering leader.
